---
title: "STAT 293 Final Project"
author: "Chuanman Li, Pei-Ling Lin and Xinhao Qu"
date: "2026-03-08"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r include=FALSE}
library(mice)
library(spdep)
library(spatialreg)
library(data.table)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(naniar)
```


```{r}
load("housing.rdata")
dat <- housing_data
```

```{r}
# =========================================================
# Missing Data Summary
# =========================================================
# install.packages("naniar")

miss_var_summary(dat)
```

```{r}
# =========================================================
# Plot Missing Data by Locations
# =========================================================
vars_to_check <- c("cmedv", "crim", "zn", "indus", "chas", "nox",
                   "rm", "age", "dis", "rad", "ptratio", "lstat")

missing_long <- dat %>%
  select(lon, lat, all_of(vars_to_check)) %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(vars_to_check), ~ is.na(.))) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(vars_to_check),
    names_to = "variable",
    values_to = "is_missing"
  )

ggplot(missing_long, aes(x = lon, y = lat)) +
  geom_point(color = "grey85", size = 1) +
  geom_point(
    data = subset(missing_long, is_missing),
    color = "red",
    size = 1,
    alpha = 0.8
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ variable) +
  labs(
    title = "Missing value locations by variable",
    x = "Longitude",
    y = "Latitude"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

```{r}
# =========================================================
# 1. Setup imputation
# =========================================================
init <- mice(dat, maxit = 0)
meth <- init$method
pred <- init$predictorMatrix

# variable-specific imputation methods
meth["chas"]  <- "logreg"   # binary
meth["rad"]   <- "polyreg"  # categorical/discrete
meth["cmedv"] <- "pmm"      # continuous target

# remove ID-like variables and medv
pred[, c("town", "tract", "medv")] <- 0

# run MI
imputed_data_set <- mice(
  dat,
  m = 20,
  method = meth,
  predictorMatrix = pred,
  maxit = 10,
  seed = 123
)

# =========================================================
# 2. SAR model
# =========================================================

# for W matrix
# Extract coordinates
coords <- cbind(dat$lon, dat$lat)

# Define neighbors (using a distance threshold or k-nearest)
ids <- knearneigh(coords, k = 5) # 5 nearest neighbors
nb <- knn2nb(ids)

# Calculate distances for those neighbors
dist_list <- nbdists(nb, coords)

# Apply Inverse Distance function: w = 1/d
inv_dist_list <- lapply(dist_list, function(x) 1/x)

# Convert to a listw object (style="W" performs Row Normalization)
# Row normalization ensures the sum of weights for each row equals 1
listw <- nb2listw(nb, glist = inv_dist_list, style = "W")

fit_sar_model <- function(data) {
  lagsarlm(
    cmedv ~ crim + zn + indus + chas + nox + rm + age +
      dis + rad + tax + ptratio + b + lstat,
    data = data,
    listw = listw
  )
}

# =========================================================
# 3. Rubin pooling for vector parameters
#    This pools all coefficients jointly, including rho
# =========================================================
pool_vector_rubin <- function(est_mat, var_mat_list) {
  m <- ncol(est_mat)
  p <- nrow(est_mat)
  
  qbar <- rowMeans(est_mat)
  
  # average within-imputation covariance
  ubar <- Reduce(`+`, var_mat_list) / m
  
  # between-imputation covariance
  b <- matrix(0, p, p)
  for (i in 1:m) {
    d <- est_mat[, i] - qbar
    b <- b + tcrossprod(d)
  }
  b <- b / (m - 1)
  
  # total covariance
  tmat <- ubar + (1 + 1 / m) * b
  se <- sqrt(diag(tmat))
  
  out <- data.frame(
    term = rownames(est_mat),
    estimate = qbar,
    std.error = se,
    row.names = NULL
  )
  out
}

# =========================================================
# 4. Primary MAR analysis
# =========================================================
sar_analyses_mar <- lapply(1:imputed_data_set$m, function(i) {
  single_set <- complete(imputed_data_set, i)
  fit_sar_model(single_set)
})

beta_mat_mar  <- sapply(sar_analyses_mar, coef)
vcov_list_mar <- lapply(sar_analyses_mar, vcov)

pooled_beta_mar <- pool_vector_rubin(beta_mat_mar, vcov_list_mar)

cat("\n--- Primary MAR pooled results ---\n")
print(pooled_beta_mar)

cat("\n--- Pooled rho under MAR ---\n")
print(subset(pooled_beta_mar, term == "rho"))

# =========================================================
# 5. Sensitivity analysis: delta-adjusted pattern-mixture
#    Shift only originally missing cmedv values
# =========================================================
miss_cmedv <- is.na(dat$cmedv)

apply_delta_cmedv <- function(data_completed, miss_index, delta) {
  data_new <- data_completed
  data_new$cmedv[miss_index] <- data_new$cmedv[miss_index] + delta
  data_new
}

# choose delta values using SD of observed cmedv
sd_cmedv <- sd(dat$cmedv, na.rm = TRUE)
deltas <- c(-0.3, -0.1, 0, 0.1, 0.3) * sd_cmedv

sens_results_beta <- vector("list", length(deltas))
names(sens_results_beta) <- as.character(deltas)

for (dd in deltas) {
  sar_analyses_delta <- lapply(1:imputed_data_set$m, function(i) {
    single_set <- complete(imputed_data_set, i)
    single_set_delta <- apply_delta_cmedv(single_set, miss_cmedv, dd)
    fit_sar_model(single_set_delta)
  })
  
  beta_mat_delta  <- sapply(sar_analyses_delta, coef)
  vcov_list_delta <- lapply(sar_analyses_delta, vcov)
  
  pooled_beta_delta <- pool_vector_rubin(beta_mat_delta, vcov_list_delta)
  pooled_beta_delta$delta <- dd
  
  sens_results_beta[[as.character(dd)]] <- pooled_beta_delta
}

sens_beta_tab <- rbindlist(sens_results_beta, fill = TRUE)

cat("\n--- Sensitivity analysis pooled results ---\n")
print(sens_beta_tab)

cat("\n--- Sensitivity analysis: rho only ---\n")
print(subset(sens_beta_tab, term == "rho"))
```
